items.summary: 5G telecommunications, according to industry hype when 5G first launched in 2019 , was going to be all about buzzy applications like mobile augmented reality and autonomous vehicles . But the surprise plot twist came when replacing home cable internet turned into 5G’s most widely adopted new application. Fixed...

items.title: The Hidden 6G Bottleneck: RF Hardware Design Is Becoming a Strategic Race
items.summary: As 5G-Advanced matures and 6G research moves closer to implementation, the wireless industry faces a deeper challenge than spectrum, standards or AI-native network architecture. Future wireless systems will depend on whether the industry can design, validate and manufacture increasingly complex RF modules fast enough.

items.title: 6G in Dalian: What the Latest 3GPP Meetings Reveal About the Future Radio and Network
items.summary: The 6G physical layer is starting to converge. The protocol stack is being simplified in meaningful places. But the most consequential architecture decisions are now moving toward the June plenary in Singapore.

items.title: T-Mobile eyes C-band, 2.7 GHz to shore up 5G edge
items.summary: T-Mobile CEO Srini Gopalan says it’s too early to tell which one will be more attractive.

items.title: Dell’Oro: Global RAN market finds stability as 5G, AI-RAN and early 6G investments take hold
items.summary: After a sharp multi-year downturn, the global RAN market is showing signs of recovery, with Dell’Oro Group forecasting modest growth through 2030 driven by 5G, AI-RAN and early 6G.

items.title: FCC sets up next C-band auction for 2027
items.summary: "America’s leadership in wireless is back," says Brendan Carr, FCC Chairman, as he announces 160 MHz of upper C‑Band to go to auction in 2027.

items.title: Ookla, Opensignal give T-Mobile more network ammo
items.summary: Two competing research firms came out with similar findings this week, each awarding top network honors to T-Mobile.

items.title: Ericsson, LG Uplus target network-based voice AI for smarter calling
items.summary: Ericsson and LG Uplus are moving voice AI deeper into telecom networks, but have their work cut out for them to overcome privacy, latency and consent hurdles.

items.title: Fierce Network opens submissions for expanded 2026 Innovation Awards program
items.summary: Fierce Network has opened submissions for its 2026 Innovation Awards, introducing 14 new categories and bringing the awards ceremony to Broadband Nation Expo for the first time as it expands its focus on AI, network automation, cloud infrastructure and broadband innovation.

items.title: AMD's Helios bets AI networking on open Ethernet
items.summary: AMD launches its Helios AI rack on open Ethernet networking, claiming 30% better token economics than Nvidia as gigawatt customer deals mount.

items.title: Nokia bets on AI supercycle as demand drives strong second quarter
items.summary: Nokia's AI and cloud networking business more than doubled in Q2, helping the vendor beat expectations even as it ramps up a costly restructuring effort.

items.title: SK Telecom spins up SK Hyper for 15GW AI data center buildout
items.summary: Korean operator SK Telecom launched a new subsidiary dedicated to its AI data center ambitions.

items.title: T-Mobile touts hot growth runway as Q2 account additions cool
items.summary: T-Mobile’s Q2 results include a 2% year-over-year growth in ARPA and a 13% decrease year-over-year in postpaid net account additions.

items.title: 'AI tailwind' keeps Arista at No. 1 spot in data center switching — now Nvidia looms
items.summary: Arista leveraged the AI buildout to take the No. 1 spot from Cisco in data center switching. Now it's facing off against the Nvidia AI colossus.

items.title: e& UAE and Core42 team to launch sovereign AI infrastructure
items.summary: The telco is teaming up with AI infrastructure provider Core42 to offer sovereign cloud services to enterprises in the UAE.
